entry.title: SAP Inside Track Wroclaw 2025 - update regarding registration of participants
entry.content:
Hello All,

Short update regarding registration on SAP Inside Track 2025 Wroclaw - we closed call for speakers and now we are preparing all materials for internet page.

So long story short - registration for participants will start on 13.05.2025! Be prepared! 🙂

entry.title: Auto propose amount in Allocated/Residual amount for Partial payment of supplier/customer
entry.content:
Hi all,

My client has a requirement to have an auto propose amount functionality in Allocated/Residual amount for Partial payment of supplier/customer. In SAP On-premises, in F-44, if choosing Partial payment tab, then double clicking on Payment amount, then amount will be auto proposed to make zero balance. 

In Public cloud, do we have similar functionality? For example, we expect in Reprocess Bank Statement Items, when double clicking on Allocated amount, it proposes the amount to make zero balance. But I don't see it now.

entry.title: Run Foreign Trade Regulations Z4
entry.content:
Hello,

Can anyone help here?

Application: Run Foreign Trade Regulations Z4.

The customer pulls an XML file from the SAP S/4 Hana Public Cloud, but it contains missing data and cannot be sent to the Federal Office.

Description of the problem:
Data is missing that was actually created in customising for the company codes.

The following data is missing and is not contained in the XML file:
Economic sector
Company address
Telephone number
Contact person
E-mail address
Company number
---> although this data is set in customising (foreign trade and balance of payments). (see screen1 and 2)

In addition, the XML structure does not contain a field for the commodity code.
Is there a way to insert a commodity code field in this XML file structure? If so, how?

Many thanks in advance.

Yours sincerely
N.S.

We are getting a block on all our sales documents + Quotes even if the customer have not yet reached the limit.

We have recently migrated all customer orders/quotes because of a Roll-Out

I checked on FD32

OVA8

FBL5N:

We have recently migrated all customer orders/quotes because of a Roll-Out

What do I need to change?

 

Many Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Re: Unexpected Exchange Rate Difference and Zero-Balance...
entry.content:
Hello dear user,

And thank you for asking your question in the SAP Community blog.

In order to analyse your issue, could you please provide the following information :

- when the customer invoice has been created ?

- what is the effective date of payment ?

- I'm supposing you're working on S4 Hana Public Cloud version. Could you please confirm it ?

Thank you in advance for your feedback.

Best regards,

Jeremy

entry.title: SAP CPQ 2505 release - "Custom Actions" option is missing in Q2.0 environment
entry.content:
Post 2505 release, unable to find "Custom Actions" option under SAP CPQ Setup -> Develop.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: SAP CPQ 2505 release - "Custom Actions" option is mis...
entry.content:
Please refer to the below point under What's New Viewer for SAP CPQ | SAP Help Portal

"The consolidated setup page for Quote Actions streamlines the process by merging custom and standard actions into a single location under the Workflow / Approval section. This integration simplifies navigation and management, making it easier to maintain and configure quote-related actions efficiently.".

So now you can find the custom actions along with the standard actions under Setup -> Workflow/Approval -> Actions.

entry.title: Re: Library view - price and item description
entry.content: Yes, but there is no debug code for that field displayed. The debug codes are only visible at the Learning administration. I have also found the Text in Manage Languages on the plattform under "pfs_search_svc_price_estimated_free". I have changed that, but it has no effect on the learning experience.

entry.title: How to Check User T-Code/Tile Usage (Last 2 Months)
entry.content:
Dear SAP Team,


We need to check which T-Codes or Fiori tiles were used by user in the last 2 months.


Kindly let us know how we can view this data — is there any specific tile, report, or method available?


Please share the steps or guidance as soon as possible.


Regards,


Udayraj K


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to Check User T-Code/Tile Usage (Last 2 Months)
entry.content:
Hi

Kindly refer the below KBA

3212871 - How to get "Identity and Access Management" information - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

You can use the "I_IAMBusinessUserLogonDetails" view to check Business User Logon Details.

While it is not possible to track which user accessed a specific app, you can monitor the applications that business users are authorized to access in the S/4HANA Cloud system using the IAM Information System app

Steps to follow:

  1. Set the "Main Entity" as "Application"
  2. Click on "Application - Business User" tab
  3. Search with the business user's name
  4. You can get the list of applications which are used by this business user

You can also find more details on SAP Help Portal - IAM Information System.

entry.title: New Member - Hello from Shubham
entry.content:
Hello everyone!

My name is Shubham, and I’m based in India. I work in the BTP team as a Technical Consultant. I'm excited to be part of this community and look forward to learning, sharing, and collaborating with all of you!

Thank you !!!

entry.title: Re: Connecting a remote SQL Anywhereto an system SAP usin...
entry.content:
Basically, there are two ways to do it:

  1. Define the SQL Anywhere as a "secondary database" in the DB setup of the SAP System.
    The people who know how to do this, would be the DB Interface team, but there is no tag for this component in SAP Community?! I have added the "Basis Technology" tag to this question. Perhaps someone from that area can provide more details on how secondary databases work.
    (Or you could search SAP Help with the keyword "secondary database".)

  2. The following may not be the best, if you want to transfer huge tables from the SQL Anywhere DB to ABAP, but if you just want to make some standard SELECTs and get the result set back into your ABAP program, the following would be a quite easy possibility:
    1. Install SAP BC (either on the same host, where the DB runs, or on one of the SAP System app-servers)
      Download link: https://support.sap.com/sbc-download
      Documentation: ​https://support.sap.com/en/product/connectors/bc/details.html?anchorId=section_295234589
    2. Define an RFC Listener for the SAP System
      (Described in the SAPAdapterGuide, chapter 4)
    3. Get a JDBC driver for SQL Anywhere and define a DB Alias for this DB
      (Described in the AdministrationGuide, chapter 14)
    4. Optionally: if there is a small number of predefined SQL statements or stored procedures, that you want to execute on the DB, you can generate "Services" that execute precisely one SQL statement/stored procedure. Otherwise you can use the "execSQL Service", which takes a generic SQL statement as input and executes it on the DB. Then you will be able to execute any SQL statement from your ABAP program, but you'll have to be careful about SQL injection.
      (See also AdministrationGuide, chapter 14)
    5. In ABAP, define one or more RFC-enabled function modules, that have a suitable table parameter for holding the result set of such an SQL statement. (Or "mis-use" existing function modules: for example, if the USERLIST table of BAPI_USER_GETLIST has a suitable layout for holding the result set of your SQL statement, then you can simply use BAPI_USER_GETLIST for fetching data from your DB... 🙂 )
    6. In the Business Connector, define Inbound Maps for these function modules to the Services created in step 4.
      (See also SAPAdapterGuide, chapter 4)
      If you are "mis-using" existing FMs, then you may need to map the fields of the DB table to the fields of the FM's table (like the USERLIST table mentioned above). See the DeveloperGuide for how field mappings (or more complicated operations on the data) can be done in the graphical editor.
    7. Now in your ABAP program, you can get an internal table with the result set from your SQL Anywhere by simply calling
      CALL FUNCTION 'XYZ' DESTINATION 'SAP_BC'

      This may look like a long list, but each step only takes a few minutes, so a basic proof of concept can probably be done in less than an hour.

entry.title: Re: Migrate the Test case and result from OpenText ALM to...
entry.content: SAP Note/KBA 3604644 does not exist. Please give more info about on this

entry.title: Re: Auto propose amount in Allocated/Residual amount for...
entry.content:
Hello dear user,

And thank you for asking your question in the SAP Community blog.

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, the automatic proposal of allocated/residual amount (as in F-44 in SAP ECC or On-Premise) when handling partial payments or clearing especially in the "Reprocess Bank Statement Items" app is not fully available out of the box.

Key Differences:

- In SAP S/4HANA On-Premise, transaction F-44 offers the auto-propose payment amount feature during partial clearing, especially when double-clicking on the amount. This behavior is tied to the SAP GUI's dynamic field behavior.

- In the Public Cloud Edition, this transactional behavior is streamlined via Fiori apps like:

    • Reprocess Bank Statement Items
    • Manage Customer Line Items
    • Manage Supplier Line Items
    • Post Incoming Payments

These Fiori apps are designed for guided processes but may lack the dynamic interaction of GUI-based transactions such as automatically proposing the balancing amount.

 

I recommend checking the latest SAP Help Portal and What's New Viewer for your system version (e.g., 2502) to see if any updates have introduced such functionality.

 

I hope this will help you answer your question.

Best regards,

Jeremy

entry.title: Re: How to Show All Receipts Across Multiple POS Systems...
entry.content:
Hallo, 

Receipts created from POS 1 can be returned or reversed from POS 2.
Also receipts parked from POS1 can be fetched in POS2 and continued.

But in the standard solution it is not possible to view in POS2, receipts that were created from POS1.
You can think of developing this using PlugIns and receipts API(also used by the Receipt APP in SAP Customer Checkout manager). But its not part of standard solution.

Hope it helps.

If your question in answered correctly, kindly select the answer as "Right Answer". This helps others to follow and easily locate answers.

Best Regards,
Bikash Bansal
Product Owner, SAP Customer Checkout

can you please suggest a way to stop the validation to effect other apps.

Habeeb

Is it correct to say that the calculated measure is set for the image?

We apologize for the inconvenience, but could you please confirm this?

entry.title: Re: Vendor Report by IO Type and Clearing Date
entry.content:
Hello dear user,

And thank you for asking your question in the SAP Community blog.

In SAP ECC 6.0, there is no single standard report that directly lists vendor invoices and credit memos by:

  • Internal Order (IO)
  • IO Type
  • Clearing Date (i.e., Payment Date)

However, you can get close by using or combining a few standard transactions:

1. Transaction: FBL1N – Vendor Line Item Display

  • Use this report with the following filters:
    • Document Type: KR (Invoice), KG (Credit Memo)
    • Clearing Date: fill in your desired range
    • Selection Criteria: Enter Internal Order under "Dynamic Selections" (KOSTL – Cost Center / AUFNR – Internal Order, depending on your posting)
    • Status: "Cleared Items" or "All Items" (to include payment date)
  • Output: Includes document number, amount, clearing document (payment), and you can add the Internal Order via the layout.

 

2. Transaction: KOB1 – Actual Line Items for Orders

  • Filters by:
    • Internal Order or Order Type
    • Posting Date or Fiscal Period
  • Shows all costs posted to an order, including vendor invoices
  • Limitation: Does not show clearing (payment) date

 

3. Custom Report / SQVI Join (Recommended workaround)

If standard reports are not sufficient, you can:

  • Use SQVI (QuickViewer) or SE16N/SE11 joins
  • Join tables like:
    • BSAK (Cleared Vendor Items) or BSIK (Open Vendor Items)
    • RBKP (Invoice Document Header)
    • RSEG (Invoice Document Items)
    • AUFK / AUFK-AUTYP (Internal Order Type)
    • BKPF / BSEG (Accounting data for clearing/payment info)
  • Extract IO, IO type, Invoice/Credit Memo, and Clearing Date

 

I hope this will help you answer your question.

Best regards,

Jeremy

entry.title: Re: How to delete null entries/values during calculation...

entry.title: Make fields non editable in Freight Order
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

In a Freight Order under General data tab, we have two fields -T ruck Capacity (Mass) and Truck Capacity (Volume). These fields are editable at the user end. Is there any way we could mark this field as non-editable. It should display the values but the user should not be able to edit it.

Please help!

entry.title: How to Trigger a Process in SAP Build Process Automation while Using Action Group and form
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I’m working with SAP Build Process Automation, and I’ve created a process that includes an Action Group to fetch external data, which I intend to use to populate fields in a Form. I’ve set up the Action Group to retrieve the data, and the form to display this data to the user.

My question is: once the process is deployed, how do I actually trigger this process to start? Specifically:

Do I need to set up a trigger?

How do I configure the trigger for this process to begin?

What type of trigger is recommended for a scenario where data is fetched externally and then shown in a form?

For context, my process should:

Fetch data from an external system via an Action Group.

Populate a field in the Form with the fetched data.

Allow the user to submit the form after the data is populated.

entry.title: Re: From IT Support to SAP Consultant: A Learner’s Evolution
entry.content: Great story Charles! An inspiration to us all!

Malvika

entry.title: Re: Step-by-Step Guide to Creating a Logical Database in SAP ABAP
entry.content:
IMPORTANT to say first: the Logical Database technology is obsolete since 2000, it's not recommended, it's not vital and according to me it's efficiently replaced by the powerful CDS views.

It's completely dead.

Your blog post will help people who need to maintain legacy code based on logical database (rare except maybe HR?), so thank you for them.

Best regards, 

Kavya

entry.title: Re: Enable Email Notification in Notification Channel Framework
entry.content:
Hello,

this is very fine. I activated the notifications for a standard workflow with success (using transaction SWF_PUSH_NOTIF1). Now I want to generate addionally Emails for this standard Workflows.
But for Standard Workflows the provider SAP_BUSINESS_WORKFLOW is used with provider class CL_SWF_PUSH_NOTIF_PROVIDER. In this case the method GET_NOTIFICATION_TYPE does not set IS_EMAIL_ENBLD to abap_true. Therefore no possibilty to sent the emails are availiable (Compare settings in fiori launchpad).
Is there any simple way to activate these additionally emails?
I could  create a new custom class using GET_NOTIFICATION_TYPE as a super class and redefine GET_NOTIFICATION_TYPE? Is there any other possibility?
Thanks, Florian

Abhinav L

entry.title: How to assign Contact Person - Partner in already migrated Customer(using LTMC)?
entry.content:
We have loaded Customer master using Migrate your data including Sales area and Sales partner data. Next, we created Contact person BP and assigned relationship with Customer. Now when we are trying to load that Contact person as Sales partner with Partner Role CP/AP using Customer - Extension by org level template of LTMC, it is now allowing us to load it. Throwing obvious error as Sales area already available in target system. 

Is there any way with which we can load Contact person as a Sales partner in S/4 system?

TIA

entry.title: Re: SAP Sales Credit Management Block

entry.title: Re: GR55 opens excel sheet while executing
entry.content:
Hi @SM1970 ,

The Excel integration needs to be deactivated.

This can be done using Environment->Options->Office Integration->Inactive:

Note: if you get an empty excel sheet and want to correct this issue, please check KBA 1155527 - ReportWriter: Office Integration-Common Customer Issues - SAP for Me

I hope it helps.

BR,

Filipe

entry.title: Re: SAP Sales Credit Management Block
entry.content: Thanks Lakshmipathi, only the open orders have been migrated that were not sent out yet

entry.title: SAP Cloud ERP Private: A Smarter Way to Simulate and Plan FUE-Based Licensing
entry.content:
Introduction

SAP offers the free STAR service to help customers analyze their existing SAP ECC system data and estimate the required Full Usage Equivalents (FUEs) for transitioning to an SAP Cloud ERP Private Edition (formerly known as RISE with SAP). This service provides an initial overview of licensing needs based on technical analysis and user classification.

The Problem

While the STAR service is a helpful starting point, its output is limited to a static PDF report. Many customers quickly realize the need for more dynamic tools to simulate different licensing scenarios, optimize FUE usage, and align recommendations with their internal understanding of user roles and authorizations. Without such capabilities, license planning becomes a one-time snapshot rather than a flexible, iterative process.

Our Approach

The Accelerator for SAM4U Adoption, available on the SAP Store, provides an enhanced and hands-on alternative. It includes:

  • SAP SAM4U – SAP’s official Software Asset Management tool designed for evaluating consolidated SAP system landscapes. It uses the same rule sets applied in the STAR service to analyze data from customer systems.

  • Extended Simulation and Reporting Tool – A powerful add-on built with SAP technologies that enables customers to run multiple STAR-like simulations themselves. By leveraging real usage data extracted via the SAM4U extractor, organizations gain control over licensing insights and optimization strategies without relying solely on a static report.

How It Works

Start by requesting access to the Accelerator for SAM4U Adoption via the SAP Store, or use the public demo tenant for a hands-on introduction.

Once data from the SAP Customer's system is uploaded into SAM4U (see the excellent official documentation or our previous blog posts for guidance), customers can create multiple consolidation scenarios. For example:

  • Deep Simulation – where more roles are reviewed and potentially optimized

  • Light Simulation – where minimal changes are applied

These simulations represent different levels of role adjustment to evaluate how they impact the projected Full Usage Equivalents (FUEs).

 Pic. 1 – SAM4U Consolidations for Deep and Light Simulations

After creating the consolidations in SAM4U, the data is automatically transferred to the Extended Reporting and Simulation Solution, typically within a few minutes.

When you open the extended simulation tool, you'll find the newly created consolidations ready for review:

 Pic. 2 – Consolidations in SAM4U Extended Reporting & Simulation Solution

As seen above, both consolidations start with the same baseline: 631 FUEs, calculated by the SAM4U engine.

Let’s now dive into the Deep Simulation scenario and evaluate the roles we might adjust—either by removing them from certain users or downgrading their classification levels.

 Pic. 3 – Choose Consolidation for Deep Simulation

Navigate to the Authorizations Analysis section to identify roles and profiles that are strong candidates for optimization:

 Pic. 4 – Deep Simulation Roles Selection

Here’s where our approach diverges from SAP’s STAR service. Customers typically know which roles are critical to their operations—even those that technically fall under the "Professional" category based on just 1–2 key authorization objects. These are often not realistic candidates for removal, despite what static analysis suggests.

And this is where our tool shines: it enables customers to make optimization decisions based on their own knowledge of business processes—something no external service can replicate.

For instance, in the Deep Simulation, let’s assume the customer chooses to optimize all high-potential roles, even though one of them, Z:SAP_MM_IM_GOODS_MOVEMENTS, is known to be widely used. While this role may be required for many users, not all fully leverage its professional-level authorizations—making it a valid candidate for deeper analysis.

 Pic. 5 – Deep Simulation Role Optimization

(For a detailed walkthrough on how to optimize roles using this solution, see our previous blog posts.)

Now, let’s look at the result after applying changes—such as removing unused authorizations or adjusting ACTVT values (e.g., from 01 - Create to 03 - Display):

 Pic. 6 – Deep Simulation Results

The result: a significant drop from 631 to 306 FUEs. This represents the lowest theoretical licensing footprint under the current authorization model—valuable for contract negotiations or long-term license planning.

Next, we switch to the Light Simulation, this time excluding Z:SAP_MM_IM_GOODS_MOVEMENTS from optimization:

 Pic. 7 – Light Simulation Roles Selection

Let’s review the outcome:

 Pic. 8 – Light Simulation Results

Here, the FUE count drops from 631 to 534 FUEs—illustrating the impact of selectively optimizing only lower-risk roles.

Conclusion

By using the Accelerator for SAM4U Adoption available on the SAP Store, customers can take a proactive and flexible approach to FUE planning. The tool allows you to:

  • Establish a baseline FUE projection (e.g., 631 FUEs)

  • Explore deep optimization scenarios that can potentially reduce FUEs to the lowest theoretical licensing footprint under the current authorization model (e.g., 306 FUEs)

  • Evaluate lighter optimization strategies that offer meaningful reductions (e.g., 534 FUEs) by safely adjusting underutilized authorizations

  • Collaborate effectively with internal authorization teams who best understand business needs and SAP usage patterns

This simulation-driven approach turns licensing analysis from a one-time event into a continuous, data-informed process—giving SAP customers more control, accuracy, and confidence in their transition to SAP Cloud ERP Private Edition.

entry.title: Unable to debug Third Party ALES Process flow in SM58
entry.content:
Hi Team,

To troubleshoot the third-party ALES process flow in the same SAP instance, we followed the steps outlined in the SAP Help Portal article "How to debug Third-party ALES process flow | SAP Help Portal." However, when we accessed SM58, we noticed an entry for the function module PUR_ORDER_CREATE_VIA_SD_EVENT. Yet, when we click on this entry and select "Debug LUW," it disappears from the screen.

Could anyone please assist ? Are there any additional steps we need to take to debug this scenario?

Regards,

Mythily

entry.title: Re: Bank Statement upload using Web Services

entry.title: Re: How to Show All Receipts Across Multiple POS Systems...
entry.content: Right Answer

entry.title: Re: Clean core _Is appending fixed domain values to stand...
entry.content:
Hello,
You can set the ABAP language version for the append domain in ADT to "ABAP for Cloud Development" to make it clean core compatible. "ABAP for Cloud Development" repository objects are safe regarding future system upgrades.
Regards,
Patrick

entry.title: Re: Disable selection of item based on a condition in SAP RAP
entry.content: Hi Sudha, I have similar requirement, could you please help and suggest how did you achieved via UI5 frontend. Please help as its really critical requirement for me. Thanks,

entry.title: Express Node.js Multi-Tenant Backend Application with XSUAA on BTP - Non CAP based
entry.content:
Hi community,

I have created a Multi tenant SAAS application (Non-CAP NodeJS based) and have deployed this app to SAP BTP in the Provider subaccount. Along with the application I have the saas-registry instance and xsuaa instance created in my Provider subaccount. I am also able to subscribe to this application from the Consumer subaccount.


Current Status: So far, I have successfully implemented application subscription from tenant subaccounts using the saas-registry and an xsuaa instance. I’ve also implemented the necessary logic for subscribe and unsubscribe events in the backend.

Note: This is a pure backend application, and I am not using an Approuter, as there is no frontend component involved. The application is intended to be consumed by tenant-specific backend or frontend apps via API calls.

Problems:

  1. When I subscribe app in consumer subaccount, the xsuaa instance (using tenant-mode as "shared in provider subaccount) is not automatically created in consumer subaccount.
  2. To address this, I added event for getting dependencies while subscribing in backend the code is shown in below picture.
  3. Also added "url" for "getDependencies" in "appUrls" and dependencies detail in SaaS configuration file as shown below.
  4. After these changes, I tried subscribing to the app again from the consumer subaccount got an error shown below.

Asking for Suggestions:

  1. How can I achieve the automatic creation of an XSUAA instance in the consumer subaccount when subscribing to the app, to ensure full isolation between consumer subaccounts?
  2. If not then how can I achieve independent OAuth 2.0 Authentication for tenants specific to their subaccount?

entry.title: Auto Clearing of Vendor Open Items using BAPI "POSTING_INTERFACE_CLEARING" with residual items too
entry.content:
Hi Folks,

I got a requirement for my client to auto clear vendor open items through a background job report. It is an alternative to standard T-Code FB1K. In FB1K user able to perform clearing of open items only for one Account at a time and it is very tedious process to match open items and related invoices and clear them by creating Residual Items too if payment is not 0. So We came up with an Z report. 

Criteria to get Open Items (lt_open_items):

BSIK records: conditions: UMSKZ should be 'A', BSCHL should be '29' 

Criteria to get Related Invoices (lt_invoices):

BSIK records: conditions: BSCHL should be '31' 

Based on lt_open_items LIFNR, BUDAT, EBELN and I matching records with EKBE & MKPF to get EBELN and updating it in lt_invoices. Everything is fine & I am able to fetch records for example as shown below:
Open Items:

BukrsBelnrGjahrbuzeiumskzbschlwrbtrwaerslifnrebelnbudatbldat
10005000202310A29100000INRVEND0011234501-01-202402-01-2024
10005002202310A29200000INRVEND0021234601-01-202402-01-2024

Invoices Data:

BukrsBelnrGjahrbuzeiumskzbschlwrbtrwaerslifnrebelnbudatbldat 
10006000202310A31500000INRVEND0011234510-01-202411-01-20241st Invoice cleared against 100000 balance is 500000
10006001202310A34300000INRVEND0011234514-01-202415-01-2024500000 is cleared against 2nd invoice residual item 200000 is created
10006002202310A31200000INRVEND0011234518-01-202419-01-2024200000 is adjusted against 3rd invoice


Challenges which i am facing now:
1. I am processing each open Item and processing it for each Unique PO through BAPI but SY-SUBRC is becoming 0 but clearing not happening and residual items are not getting created obviously because somewhere I am loosing it.


ABAP LOGIC:
    TYPES: BEGIN OF ty_awkey,
             belnr TYPE belnr_d,
             gjahr TYPE gjahr,
           END OF ty_awkey.

    DATA: lt_awkey TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF ty_awkey WITH EMPTY KEY.

    DATA: lt_return TYPE TABLE OF bapiret2,
          lv_line   TYPE i,
          lv_index  TYPE sy-tabix,
          lv_flag   TYPE flag.

    DATA: ls_header   TYPE bapiache09,
          lt_currency TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF bseg,
          lt_ftclear  TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF ftclear,
          lt_ftpost   TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF ftpost,
          ls_ftclear  TYPE ftclear,
          lt_blntab   TYPE TABLE OF blntab,
          lt_fttax    TYPE TABLE OF fttax,
          ls_ftpost   TYPE ftpost,
          lv_count(3) TYPE n VALUE '001',
          lv_simu     TYPE char1,
          lv_function TYPE funct_pi VALUE 'C',
          lv_mode     TYPE allgazmd VALUE 'N',
          lv_balance  TYPE wrbtr.


    SELECT bukrs, belnr, gjahr, buzei, umskz, bschl, blart,
           wrbtr, waers,lifnr, ebeln, budat, bldat
       FROM bsik
       INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_source_doc)
       WHERE lifnr IN @s_lifnr
       AND bukrs IN @s_bukrs
       AND budat LE @p_date
       AND umskz EQ 'A'
       AND bschl EQ '29'
      ORDER BY lifnr, ebeln, budat.
    IF sy-subrc NE 0.
      MESSAGE TEXT-t01 TYPE 'I'.
      LEAVE LIST-PROCESSING.
    ELSE.
      SORT lt_source_doc BY lifnr ebeln budat.
    ENDIF.

    SELECT bukrs, belnr, gjahr, buzei, umskz, bschl, blart,
           wrbtr, waers,lifnr, ebeln,budat
       FROM bsik
       INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_recipient_doc)
       WHERE lifnr IN @s_lifnr
       AND bukrs IN @s_bukrs
       AND budat LE @p_date
       AND bschl EQ '31'
      ORDER BY lifnr, ebeln, budat.
    IF sy-subrc NE 0.
      MESSAGE TEXT-t02 TYPE 'I'.
      LEAVE LIST-PROCESSING.
    ELSE.
      SORT lt_recipient_doc BY lifnr ebeln budat.
    ENDIF.

    IF lt_recipient_doc IS NOT INITIAL.

      SELECT bukrs, belnr, gjahr, awkey
       INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_bkpf)
       FROM bkpf
       FOR ALL ENTRIES IN @lt_recipient_doc
       WHERE bukrs EQ @lt_recipient_doc-bukrs
       AND belnr EQ @lt_recipient_doc-belnr
       AND gjahr EQ @lt_recipient_doc-gjahr.
    ENDIF.

    lt_awkey = VALUE #( FOR bk IN lt_bkpf (
                                belnr = bk-awkey(10)
                                gjahr = bk-awkey+10(4) ) ).

    IF lt_awkey IS NOT INITIAL.
      SELECT belnr, gjahr, ebeln
         INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_ekbe)
         FROM ekbe
         FOR ALL ENTRIES IN @lt_awkey
         WHERE belnr = @lt_awkey-belnr
         AND gjahr = @lt_awkey-gjahr.
    ENDIF.


    LOOP AT lt_recipient_doc ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<inv>).
      READ TABLE lt_bkpf INTO DATA(ls_bkpf)
           WITH KEY bukrs = <inv>-bukrs belnr = <inv>-belnr gjahr = <inv>-gjahr.
      IF sy-subrc = 0.
        DATA(lv_belnr) = ls_bkpf-awkey(10).
        DATA(lv_gjahr) = ls_bkpf-awkey+10(4).
        READ TABLE lt_ekbe INTO DATA(ls_ekbe)
             WITH KEY belnr = lv_belnr gjahr = lv_gjahr.
        IF sy-subrc = 0.
          <inv>-ebeln = ls_ekbe-ebeln.
        ENDIF.
      ENDIF.
    ENDLOOP.

*    SORT lt_recipient_doc BY lifnr ebeln budat.

    LOOP AT lt_source_doc INTO DATA(ls_source).
      lv_balance = ls_source-wrbtr.
      lv_flag = abap_true.

      LOOP AT lt_recipient_doc INTO DATA(ls_recepient_doc) WHERE lifnr = ls_source-lifnr
                              AND ebeln = ls_source-ebeln AND wrbtr > 0 AND waers = ls_source-waers.
        IF lv_balance LE 0.
          EXIT.
        ENDIF.

        APPEND VALUE #( agkoa = 'K'
                            agkon = ls_source-lifnr
                            agbuk = ls_source-bukrs
                            agums = ls_source-umskz
                            selfd = 'BELNR'
                            selvon = ls_source-belnr
                            selbis = ls_recepient_doc-belnr
                            xnops = abap_true ) TO lt_ftclear.

        IF lv_balance GE ls_recepient_doc-wrbtr.
          lv_balance = lv_balance - ls_recepient_doc-wrbtr.
        ELSE.
          lv_balance = 0.
        ENDIF.
      ENDLOOP.

      CLEAR: lt_blntab, lt_fttax, lt_ftpost, ls_ftpost.
      IF lv_flag IS NOT INITIAL.
        APPEND VALUE #( stype = gc_header count = lv_count fnam = 'BKPF-BLDAT' fval = ls_source-bldat )  TO lt_ftpost.
        APPEND VALUE #( stype = gc_header count = lv_count fnam = 'BKPF-BUDAT' fval = ls_source-budat )  TO lt_ftpost.
        APPEND VALUE #( stype = gc_header count = lv_count fnam = 'BKPF-BELNR' fval = ls_source-belnr )  TO lt_ftpost.
        APPEND VALUE #( stype = gc_header count = lv_count fnam = 'BKPF-BLART' fval = 'AB' )             TO lt_ftpost.
        APPEND VALUE #( stype = gc_header count = lv_count fnam = 'BKPF-BUKRS' fval = ls_source-bukrs )  TO lt_ftpost.
        APPEND VALUE #( stype = gc_header count = lv_count fnam = 'BKPF-WAERS' fval = ls_source-waers )  TO lt_ftpost.
        APPEND VALUE #( stype = gc_item   count = lv_count fnam = 'RF05A-NEWKO' fval = ls_source-lifnr ) TO lt_ftpost.
        DATA(lv_sgtxt) = |Auto clearing|.
        APPEND VALUE #( stype = gc_item   count = lv_count fnam = 'BSEG-SGTXT' fval = lv_sgtxt )         TO lt_ftpost.
        APPEND VALUE #( stype = gc_item   count = lv_count fnam = 'BSEG-BSCHL' fval = '29' )             TO lt_ftpost.
        APPEND VALUE #( stype = gc_item   count = lv_count fnam = 'BSEG-WRBTR' fval = lv_balance )       TO  lt_ftpost.
        APPEND VALUE #( stype = gc_item   count = lv_count fnam = 'RF05A-NEWUM' fval = ls_source-umskz ) TO lt_ftpost.


        CALL FUNCTION 'POSTING_INTERFACE_START'
          EXPORTING
            i_function         = lv_function
            i_mode             = lv_mode
          EXCEPTIONS
            client_incorrect   = 1
            function_invalid   = 2
            group_name_missing = 3
            mode_invalid       = 4
            update_invalid     = 5
            user_invalid       = 6
            OTHERS             = 7.
        IF sy-subrc <> 0.
* Implement suitable error handling here
        ENDIF.

        CALL FUNCTION 'POSTING_INTERFACE_CLEARING'
          EXPORTING
            i_auglv                    = 'UMBUCHNG'
            i_tcode                    = 'FB05'
            i_sgfunct                  = 'C'
*           i_xsimu                    = space
          IMPORTING
            e_msgid                    = sy-msgid
            e_msgno                    = sy-msgno
            e_msgty                    = sy-msgty
            e_msgv1                    = sy-msgv1
            e_msgv2                    = sy-msgv2
            e_msgv3                    = sy-msgv3
            e_msgv4                    = sy-msgv4
          TABLES
            t_blntab                   = lt_blntab
            t_ftclear                  = lt_ftclear
            t_ftpost                   = lt_ftpost
            t_fttax                    = lt_fttax
          EXCEPTIONS
            clearing_procedure_invalid = 1
            clearing_procedure_missing = 2
            table_t041a_empty          = 3
            transaction_code_invalid   = 4
            amount_format_error        = 5
            too_many_line_items        = 6
            company_code_invalid       = 7
            screen_not_found           = 8
            no_authorization           = 9
            OTHERS                     = 10.

        IF sy-subrc <> 0.
        ENDIF.

        CALL FUNCTION 'POSTING_INTERFACE_END'
          EXPORTING
            i_bdcimmed              = abap_true
          EXCEPTIONS
            session_not_processable = 1
            OTHERS                  = 2.
        IF sy-subrc <> 0.
* Implement suitable error handling here
        ENDIF.

        READ TABLE lt_blntab INTO DATA(ls_blntab) INDEX 1.
        IF sy-subrc = 0.
          gs_final-belnr = ls_blntab-belnr.
          gs_final-bukrs = ls_blntab-bukrs.
          gs_final-msg   = 'Document is cleared successfully'.
        ELSE.
          IF sy-msgty IS NOT INITIAL.
            MESSAGE ID sy-msgid TYPE sy-msgty NUMBER sy-msgno INTO gs_final-msg
                    WITH sy-msgv1 sy-msgv2 sy-msgv3 sy-msgv4.
          ENDIF.
        ENDIF.

      ENDIF.
      CLEAR lv_flag.
    ENDLOOP.


Please let me know, If I am making mistake any where or I am taking wrong approach:
@ABAP @SAPABAP ABAP Development @FICO #

entry.title: Re: Process design and standardization for SAP Digital Supply Chain Cloud Solutions in SAP Signavio
entry.content:
Hi Hendrik,

For EWM the process content is available as part of the Best Practice content for S/4HANA through the Process Navigator in the SAP for Me platform.  For the Digital Supply Chain solutions that I have mentioned in the blog post, they are not available in that repository yet, which is why this separate content was produced as an installed package through Signavio.

Here is a link that will help you find the content for the EWM processes.  You would need to select the "Supply Chain" LoB from the list on the left, then you will be able to see all of the scope items that are relevant to EWM.

https://me.sap.com/processnavigator/SolS/EARL_SolS-055/2023-FPS02?region=DE

Good luck!

Carson

entry.title: Re: Extract ECC
entry.content:
I would say that the best approach will be the native DBMS tool. Almost every DBMS engine have a tool to export data, examples:

  • MSSQL: bcp and SQL Server Import and Export Wizard
  • ORACLE: data pump

entry.title: Event when Workitem is generated for a User
entry.content:
Hi All, 

We are looking for a possibility to Trigger some additional Functionality whenever a Workitem is generated for a user(Irrespective of Workflows, Tasks). Information about the User who should Process the Work item must be available.

This functionality should call an External UI Application via Proxy to Notify the Application about a new Workitem for the user. 

Is there a way to achieve this ?

Br,

Ketan

entry.title: Re: why Crystal Reports doesn't support Print Dialog to b...
entry.content:
Did you enable the CR Viewers Print Button?

You can also enable the Export Button.

If you want you can hide CR's print and export button and place your own buttons to do both through code.

Just remember you cannot print to PDF directly using CR's print button, I believe this is what you want to do using your own button, export then print to PDF.

Unfortunately there is no Print button click event:

So your first suggestion to use your own button is the only option for you.

Also be aware the export is formatted for their default printer unless you add dialog boxes for them to select their printer first and then export and then print the PDF using WEB PDF API's.

Hope that's clear now...

entry.title: Analytical Insights in Joule – Setup Guide
entry.content:
We are happy to announce the General Availability of the 4th Joule conversational pattern, the most requested service: the Analytical Insights capability with Joule. This release will help our Joule users ask analytics questions and obtain the required information from the SAP Analytics Cloud tenant.

Image 1

You can take a look at the demo of the analytics pattern here or you can always look at the blog post by @Flavia_Moser: Introducing Analytical Insights in Joule: Empowering Smarter Decisions, Instantly

After the wonderful video demo, let’s explore how to activate it within your landscape. First, it’s important to understand Joule’s overall strategy – a unified user experience. Please take a few minutes to explore it yourself if you are just getting started.

****************************************************************************

This blog post is a series for Joule Activation:

  1. SAP Business AI – Overview for all !!! (Includes information on Joule-supported data Centers, Different scenarios during Joule setup, etc.)
  2. Joule - Unified Setup: Bridging Simplicity and Performance (Demo Video to understand the Unified Setup, Joule basics & architecture, how to set up Joule, etc...) 
  3. Joule for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition - A Comprehensive Setup Guide
  4. Joule Setup – End-to-End Setup Guide (for all Line of Business) Unified Approach
  • Joule for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition - Setup Guide
  • SAP Digital Supply Chain
    • Joule for SAP Integrated Business Planning (SAP IBP) – Setup Guide
  • Joule – Getting Started with Document Grounding - setup guide
  • Activate Joule with SAP Build Work Zone and SAP Mobile Start
  • Joule in SAP Signavio Process Transformation Suite – Setup Guide

👉Joule in SAP Analytics Cloud – Setup Guide – you are here

Blogs - Work in Progress (WIP):

  • SAP SuccessFactors Joule Activation (WIP)
  • SAP Digital Supply Chain (WIP)
    • SAP Asset Performance Management
    • SAP Product Lifecycle Management
    • SAP Digital Manufacturing
  • SAP Concur
  • SAP Ariba
  • SAP LeanIX
  • SAP Sales Cloud v2
  • and more to be added towards General Availability

****************************************************************************

Pre-requisites:

  • Joule Entitlements
  • SAP Analytics Cloud should be on the fast-track version (version 2025.7.0 and above; if you are still on version 2025.2, you still need to wait for your tenant’s updates)
  • Your SAP Analytics Cloud utilizes SAP Cloud Identity Services or SAML SSO, with your email address functioning as your login method. For further details, refer to SAP Note 3601035.

If you need to make any changes to your authentication with SAML SSO, you can refer to this help page: Enable a Custom SAML Identity Provider.

  • SAP Analytics Cloud – OAuth slot available for Joule setup

You can find the information in your SAC by going to System -> Administration -> App Integration and checking the Configured Clients section. If you see the “+ Add a New OAuth Client” button, then the prerequisites are fulfilled.

  • The Just Ask feature is enabled in your SAC tenant

(In case it’s not active, you can activate it via the System -> Administration -> Default Appearance -> and check that the Just Ask toggle under the Conversational Analytics.)

Hint: Always refer to the SAP Help Page, as they are updated regularly, and you can find the links here for :

- Integration with SAP Analytics Cloud

Integration Process:

In the current release of Analytical Pattern in Joule, at least one existing Joule setup is required with any line of business (LoB), such as SAP SuccessFactors, SAP S/4HANA Cloud, or SAP Build Work Zone. Joule can also be used in stand-alone mode. Please note that Joule is not currently available in your SAP Analytics Cloud tenant; this functionality is on our roadmap.

We have found that SAP customers already have Joule activated in most cases; therefore, we will address both options.

Image 2

Let us have a look at the steps involved for the integration:

  1. Integrating Joule
    • Scenario 1: Analytical Insights, a new setup
    • Scenario 2: You have an existing Joule setup, and a formation has been created
  2. Post-integration checks
  3. Joule test scenarios

Now that we have a process in place, we are ready to get started:

  1. Integrating Joule

Scenario 1: Analytical insights, a new setup

As mentioned above, for the new Joule setup with SAP Analytics Cloud, please ensure you select SAP Build Work Zone during the Booster process if you plan to activate Joule only for analytical insight capabilities.

  • You can follow the steps in this blog - Joule Setup – End-to-End Setup Guide (for all Line of Business) Unified Approach
    • Only skip the following steps
      • Step 3a. SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition – Maintain Extensions on SAP BTP
      • Step 3b. SAP SuccessFactors - Extension Center
      • Step 3c. Validate your System Registration in SAP BTP
    • Follow all the steps apart from the ones listed above and focus on the steps below:
      • In step 6 - Activate Cloud Foundry and Create a Space. Required only if you are looking to activate Joule with SAP Build Work Zone
      • In step 10 – Select Integration and Select Capabilities – please select SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP Build Work Zone

Once you’ve completed the Joule Booster process, you should be able to see the Joule formation in Ready status.

If you wish to access Joule in SAP Build Work Zone, please proceed with the activation process by following the blog:  Activate Joule with SAP Build Work Zone and SAP Mobile Start.

This completes the Joule activation for analytical insights.

 

Scenario 2: You have an existing Joule setup, and a formation has been created

In this section, we will show you how to activate analytical insights in the context of an existing Joule formation.

Within your SAP BTP Global Account, navigate to System Landscapes, click on Formations, and search for the Joule formation you have already set up. In my case, I am using one of the existing formations, which has multiple SAP products connected to one Joule instance, as shown below. You can click on Include System.

Image 3

In the new window, you may filter it only for SAP Analytics Cloud or select the desired system for Joule integration and click on Next.

Image 4

Select the option “Enable Capability Deployment” and click on Review.

Image 5 

Review the details, and if all is good, you may click on Include.

Image 6 

The process may take 5 to 8 minutes to complete, and you should be able to see the synchronizing option, as shown below. 

 Image 7

Once the status turns green and Ready, we are good to use Joule with Analytics capabilities.

Image 8

This completes the Joule activation for analytical insights.

 

2. Post-integration checks

 Once you follow the steps above, you can validate if the BTP Destination is created in your SAP BTP Subaccount. To check this, navigate to your SAP BTP Subaccount and click on Connectivity -> click on Destinations.

You should be able to see a Destination named – “DYN_SAC_SSO” with some of the details as shown below.

 

Note: No edits/changes are required; it’s a check if the setup is completed.

 

Image 9

Second, also check if the OAuth Client is created in your SAP Analytics Cloud system as shown below.

 Image 10

3. Joule test scenarios

We are now ready to set up the test models in the SAP Analytics Cloud and prepare Joule to pick the required responses. To prepare your data models, please refer to the section “Index Your Data Using Just Ask for Analytical Insights”.

Once you have indexed your models, you should be able to see the Joule response as below – please adjust according to your data.

Example: Show me an analytical chart for sales based on the month

Image 11

The above screen is from the SAP SuccessFactors Joule system, and since we have a Joule setup in a Unified setup with other SAP products, the next screen is in SAP LeanIX as an example to demonstrate that the conversation continues for the users, as shown below.

Image 12

In a similar approach, you may use Joule with a unified experience in other SAP products that you have integrated in the same subaccount.

If you have any questions about the setup, you can email us at SAP_AI_RIG@sap.com with the subject line "Joule—Analytical Insights.” 

Happy Learning!!!

Regards,

Nagesh Caparthy

entry.title: Re: How do I report on Alternative Cost Distribution in T...
entry.content:
Hi Simon,

 

OnTable Reports I think that is not possible, but I don't want to give you an incorrect information so, I keep to another experts to help you with it.

About Canvas Report, on "Add Component", select Tables and click on "List Report"

Click on "Edit Query" and in new windows, on pop-up box called "Select Query", click on "Advanced Reporting" and "New" button

What would you like to report on? select "Employment"

Below you will see the Job Information entity, click on the arrow inside of

Scroll Down until the end of the "bold" options, you will see the "Alternative Cost Center" entity on top of the list.

I hope that helped with your doubt.

 

Best Regards

JC

entry.title: Re: FREE_SELECTIONS_DIALOG - force table name in where clause?
entry.content:
I guess no. Can you do a SELECT with a join via EXISTS? e.g.

SELECT * FROM table1 WHERE (dynamic1) AND EXISTS ( SELECT * FROM table2 WHERE (join+dynamic2) )

entry.title: Changing the transaction status of a position flow without actually posting it.
entry.content:
• How to change the status of the position flow into fixed from being scheduled? 
  • The status of a position flow remains as scheduled even after manual posting.
  • The scheduled position flow is picked up in subsequent posting runs, despite having been posted previously.

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: OLE2 objects: Select several ranges at once
entry.content:
Well, as I mentioned earlier, the version of my project is very old, ABAP 701, and I copied the ABAP2XLSX solution a long time ago from other project.

I had to fix many code errors to make it work on my version, so I don't have the latest updates and I can't use the example ZDEMO_EXCEL_FILL_TEMPLATE.

In any case, the question is about an already completed development in my project, which is very large and uses OLE. The question is not about using other technologies or reengineering everything, which would take a lot of time. It's just a simple question about OLE2, to see if it's possible to select more than one range at the same time. In VBA it's very easy, but I can't manage to do it with OLE.

In the example I provided, you can quickly test it by copying and pasting: with option 1 (a single range), it works fine; with option 2 (multiple ranges), it doesn't work correctly.

entry.title: Re: Changing the transaction status of a position flow wi...
entry.content:
1. Use the App "Mark Flows as Fixed - Legacy Data Transfer" to change the status of the relevant flows to 'Fixed' without creation of an accounting document.
  2. After position flow is fixed via "Mark Flows as Fixed - Legacy Data Transfer", the position flow will have status 'Fixed' in Treasury Position Flows, and the corresponding cash flow in the deal will have posting information 'Fixed (not relevant for posting)'. 

Also provided in the SAP KBA https://me.sap.com/notes/3604775 

Thanks & regards.

entry.title: Re: ABAP Extensibility tutorial : Create a Shopping Cart...
entry.content:
First, any validation error on this DB table, missing mandatory fields...?

Second, have you filled in all the information that was needed? Navigate through the wizard tree (Business Objects, Data Model, etc…), maintain the artefact names provided in the table below, and press Next >...

entry.title: Re: WFA Starter Packages Not Loading with No import logs...
entry.content: @YSE-1 I have direct messaged you the company ID and the Data center. Please let me know if I need to resend.

entry.title: Re: M3305 The material 0 does not exist or is not activated
entry.content:
Dear All,

Today, I encountered an error message and investigated the issue. Upon checking the MSEG table, I found that some fields contain incorrect values. This was caused by the Z-Program used for uploading material documents, which calls a BAPI and fails to update certain values correctly.

If you are updating data using the Z-Program, please be aware of this issue and ensure proper validation and data handling.

Best regards,
Miw

entry.title: Is there any way to use se38 report in SAP public cloud?
entry.content:
Hi Team,

Scenario: I'm using sflight table but its delivered empty record. So, sap help portal suggesting use SAPBC_DATA_GENERATOR report. problem is SAP public cloud is not allowing to execute reports (SE38). is there any way to use SAPBC_DATA_GENERATOR?

Regards,

Nirojan.

entry.title: Re: Link to Download Crystal Reports 2020
entry.content:
Go here to get either the Trial version or purchase it, you'll get an e-mail with the Keycode to install:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_CRYSTAL_REPORTS?locale=en-US

Thanks

entry.title: Re: unable to get first name/last name in SAP analytics s...
entry.content:
Hey,
we are facing same issue. Did you solve it?

Carsten

entry.title: Retaining Customer PO Line Number at Forecast Date Level from DELFOR in SAP S/4HANA
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

Has anyone worked with customers sending DELFOR (830) forecast messages tied to Sales Order Scheduling Agreements, where there is a requirement to retain the customer’s PO line number at the forecast date level within SAP S/4HANA? 

If you’ve addressed this scenario:

  • How did you capture or persist the customer PO line number per forecast date?

  • Did you leverage any custom fields, user exits, or enhancements in the IDoc processing (e.g., in IDOC_INPUT_DELFOR)?

  • Were you able to map this directly into a standard SAP field or table for reporting and traceability?

Any examples, design patterns, or best practices would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you in advance for your insights!

entry.title: Re: manage validation for journal entry
entry.content:
Hello dear user,

And thank you for asking your question in the SAP Community blog.

Can you please provide a screenshot of your validation rule and also the message you get (if possible the reference number would be appreciated) ?

Thank you in advance for your feedback.

Best regards,

Jeremy

entry.title: Re: Avoid hardcoded cases by using joins
entry.content: The ABAP logic that pushes down to HANA should be in the right direction, so you may try the CDS function(CASE, WHEN...), if you don`t have CDS, create one... 🙂

entry.title: Re: Crystal Reports 2025 crashing after attempting to con...
entry.content:
I looked at their site and downloaded their 64 bit Client.

I see they have 2 formats:

Unicode I think would be the one you need, CR supports Unicode. You may need to test both.

I installed the client but of course I don't have access to any Server so the only thing I could do is try selecting the DSN I created without connection info, no problems with that part but couldn't go any further.

While I was on their site I search for "crystal Reports" and no hits. I highly suggest you contact their support team and ask them to test it also, they may need to "fix" something or provide steps on how to.

If they need CR tell them to go here and get the trial version:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_CRYSTAL_REPORTS?locale=en-US

There is a link on that page for supported data sources, Mongo is not list but ODBC is and CR has always supported ODBC Clients so I believe their is something not being handle in the Client that CR can't deal with.

PS - I tested this in CR 2025 and installed the Mongo 64 bit Client because CR 2025 is a 64 bit application

entry.title: Re: Digital badge didn't receive
entry.content:
Hi @diego43 
Please check the Troubleshooting SAP Universal ID
This section describes the various tools that are available for troubleshooting about SAP Universal ID, all designed to help you quickly. If you can't find the answer here, create a case - our team will be happy to help you. 
Please check there the  I have access to the email address that my SAP Certificate(s) is (are) connected to and...

In a nutshell

  • If you are an SAP Partner, Go to SAP Partner Portal Access Contact Us from the menu on the right side Choose "User Management" add the following information: • e-mail • S-User for the exam • Exam code • Proof of certification (PDF, screenshot, Credly URL) • SAP Universal ID
  • If you are an individual / SAP Customer, please create a case here 
    Select category "Learning and Certification Advice".
    Enter the following Information:
    - Email address
    - S-User taken for the exam
    - Exam Code
    - Proof of certification completion (by either pdf or screenshot or by Credly URL - SAP Universal ID Email address

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Re: Is there any way to use se38 report in SAP public cloud?
entry.content:
Hello dear user,

And thank you for asking your question in the SAP Community blog.

No, you can’t use SE38 or run custom ABAP reports in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

That’s because this version of SAP is designed to keep the core system “clean.” It doesn’t allow access to traditional tools like SE38, SE80, or SE93. You also can’t write or run your own ABAP programs directly in the system.

 

So what can you do instead?

It depends on what you were using SE38 for. Here are some common use cases and what to use in the cloud version:

If you used SE38 to...

In Public Cloud, use...

Run reports

Standard Fiori apps like Display Journal Entries or Trial Balance

Add logic or rules

Use the Manage Substitutions/Validations app or Business Rules

Get data out

Build Custom CDS Views and use them in reports or expose via OData

Write your own code

You’ll need to use SAP BTP (Business Technology Platform) for custom apps or reporting

 

If you really need to use SE38 and custom ABAP, the Private Cloud Edition of S/4HANA might be a better fit — it gives you access to all the classic SAP tools.

 

I hope this will help you answer your question.

Best regards,

Jeremy

entry.title: How do setup IAS login page for Onboardee and SSO page for Employee authentication?
entry.content:
The client has set up IAS and SSO for SuccessFactors. The expected behavior is that employees will log in via SSO, while onboardees and other users will use the IAS login process.

Current Configuration
The default authenticating identity provider is set to IAS. Conditional authentication is configured such that employee logging in with an email address ending in client domain (@xyz.com) are redirected to the SSO login page, while all other users are directed to the IAS login page where they authenticate using a username and password.
Real-time sync of onboardee is enabled in IAS

The client is not having any IP range for employee to configure in Conditional rule.

Current behaviour

All users(Employee and Onboardee) are redirecting to IAS login. Once they enter username or email address, employee redirect to SSO page and onboardee continue in IAS login page.

Client Requirement

When an employee accesses the SuccessFactors (SF) URL, they should be redirected directly to the SSO login page, bypassing the IAS login page. However, when an onboardee clicks the login link, they should be directed to the IAS login page for authentication.
Has anyone configured or designed a solution for a similar requirement?

Additionally, is the client's requirement feasible?

@jaideepshetty @zebashah @lukemarson @sushilgupta857 @abdulbasit 
SAP Single Sign-On SAP SuccessFactors Onboarding Identity Provisioning #authentication

entry.title: How to send control recipe to CPI (Cloud Platform Integration)
entry.content:
Hi

We want to send process order data from SAP to our MES system via CPI. But the RFC destination has to be a type T - TCP/IP connection. We otherwise use type G for all communication to CPI. 

How do we proceed from here?

entry.title: Re: Message server query failed with return code -14
entry.content: This seems to be wrong. Gui-Groups (without the "Ext. RFC-enabled" flag activated), can also be used for RFC logon. (Just tested with latest Kernel release 917 and JCo 3.1.12) The flag "Ext. RFC-enabled" only has an influence on whether NAT works or doesn't work. See note 593058, point 4b.

entry.title: Increased IPA File Size After Updating to Latest SAP BTP SDK for iOS Release
entry.content:
We currently have an iOS application which supports iPhone, iPad and MacOS. ( VisionOS not supported )

After updating to SAP BTP sdk version 24.12.0, we see a substantial increase in app size of our application.

Is there a way to disable VisionOS support from frameworks so app size can be reduced to previous.

entry.title: Import service categories in SAP Service Cloud v2
entry.content:
Hello,

I'm trying to import service categories, and I already create the service catalogue in the system. However, when i import the file i have an error: "Service catalogue doesn't exist". Any one knows how to solve it?

Thanks

entry.title: Re: why Crystal Reports doesn't support Print Dialog to b...
entry.content:
Hi Don!, thanks for you reply and your time, What I need is just show a browser Print Dialog when I click on print button. but CrystalReportViewer doesn't show Print dialog it forcibly export report to pdf. so the end-user can't show Print Dialog and then Print to their selected print from Browser Print Dialog like other Reporting tools. I wonder why this functionality not implemented in asp.net webform from many long time!

The Browser Print Preview dialog should appeared rather than export then user manually open pdf then open print preview then print.

entry.title: Re: SAP Integration Suite for DEV and PROD
entry.content: If you only have the one entitlement then you will be restricted to using a single subaccount.

entry.title: Re: JDBC Adapter Fails to Parse Scalar Response from Stor...
entry.content: @DhrubS the adapter processing level is one spot where you do not have the capability of intercepting, and it's basic job is to run the statement and generate an XML representation (doesn't expect it, it's responsible for generating it) of the result for which the anonymous scalar value is impossible to name appropriately. You can look into a custom adapter that mimics the JDBC adapter, but frankly that's too much wasteful work when adjusting the stored procedure takes seconds.

entry.title: Re: How do setup IAS login page for Onboardee and SSO page for Employee authentication?
entry.content:
I had similar requirement before but unfortunately didn't find any solution.

I think it is a feasible requirement but there are technical limitations. Because IAS cannot understand if the user is employee or onboardee before they enter the email address.

The only solution came into my mind was also specifying an IP range but that was also not possible in my case.

If you're struggling with any cost accounting topics, I’d definitely suggest giving them a try. Their support is super responsive, and the quality of the work helped boost my grades. Totally worth it for assignment help!

entry.title: Re: Account 64002000 requires an account assignment relev...
entry.content: Thanks for sharing your issue! I faced a similar problem while working on a cost accounting task, and I know how frustrating it can be. The error you're seeing means that the account requires a cost center or internal order assignment. When I got stuck, I reached out for Cost Accounting Assignment Help from domyaccountingassignment. com , and it really made a difference. If you're struggling too, I suggest giving them a try—they helped me understand and fix my assignment issues fast.
